evidence_inventory.title: Crisis Exists

core_papers.title: AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data
core_papers.key_finding: Models lose performance when trained on AI-generated data recursively

core_papers.title: The Curse of Recursion: Training on Generated Data Makes Models Forget
core_papers.key_finding: Recursive training causes catastrophic forgetting of original data distribution

core_papers.title: Towards Understanding the Interplay of Generative Artificial Intelligence and the Internet
core_papers.key_finding: AI-generated content increasingly pollutes internet training data

core_papers.title: Self-Consuming Generative Models go MAD
core_papers.role: Demonstrates autophagous collapse in image models
core_papers.key_finding: Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD) occurs across modalities

core_papers.title: Position: Model Collapse Does Not Mean What You Think
core_papers.key_finding: Argues collapse narrative may be overblown or misunderstood

evidence_inventory.narrative: The crisis was first established by Shumailov et al. (2024) who proved models degrade when trained recursively on synthetic data. This was confirmed across language models by the 'Curse of Recursion' paper and extended to image generation by Alemohammad et al. Martinez et al. documented how this creates a real-world contamination loop as AI content floods the internet. However, Dohmatob et al. provide important skepticism about whether the crisis is as severe as claimed.
remaining_gaps: Quantification of actual web contamination rates
Real-world impact measurements beyond lab settings

evidence_inventory.title: Empirical Evidence

core_papers.title: Collapse or Thrive? Perils and Promises of Synthetic Data in a Self-Generating World
core_papers.key_finding: Models collapse under some conditions but can thrive with proper curation

core_papers.title: On the Stability of Iterative Retraining of Generative Models on their own Data
core_papers.key_finding: Identifies critical instability thresholds in iterative retraining

core_papers.title: A Tale of Tails: Model Collapse as a Change of Scaling Laws
core_papers.role: Connects collapse to neural scaling laws
core_papers.key_finding: Model collapse fundamentally alters scaling law behavior

core_papers.title: The Power of Noise: Redefining Retrieval for RAG Systems
core_papers.role: Shows noise can prevent collapse in specific architectures
core_papers.key_finding: Controlled noise injection improves robustness against synthetic data

evidence_inventory.narrative: Empirical validation comes from multiple angles: Guo et al. provide comprehensive experiments showing collapse is conditional on data curation quality. Bertrand et al. identify specific iteration thresholds where instability emerges. Dohmatob et al. connect the phenomenon to fundamental changes in scaling laws. Cuconasu et al. demonstrate that architectural choices like noise injection can mitigate effects.

evidence_inventory.title: Theoretical Framework

core_papers.title: Heat Death of Generative Models in Closed-Loop Learning
core_papers.role: Provides thermodynamic analogy and mathematical framework
core_papers.key_finding: Models approach maximum entropy state in closed loops

core_papers.title: Self-Consuming Generative Models with Curated Data Provably Optimize Human Preferences
core_papers.role: Theoretical proof of conditions for beneficial self-consumption
core_papers.key_finding: With proper curation, self-consumption can optimize preferences

core_papers.title: Is Model Collapse Inevitable? Breaking the Curse of Recursion by Accumulating Real and Synthetic Data
core_papers.key_finding: Accumulation of real data can prevent collapse under specific conditions

evidence_inventory.narrative: The theoretical understanding centers on Seddik et al.'s thermodynamic framework showing models trend toward maximum entropy. However, Padmanabhan et al. prove that with careful curation, self-consumption can actually optimize for human preferences. Gerstgrasser et al. provide the key theoretical result that accumulating real data alongside synthetic can break the recursion curse.
remaining_gaps: Unified mathematical framework across all modalities
Precise threshold calculations for safe synthetic ratios

evidence_inventory.title: Novel Experiments

core_papers.title: AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data
core_papers.key_finding: Variance formula Var(X_j^n) = σ²(1+n/M) predicts collapse

core_papers.title: Collapse or Thrive? Perils and Promises of Synthetic Data in a Self-Generating World
core_papers.key_finding: Specific mixture ratios can maintain model quality

evidence_inventory.narrative: Key experiments should validate Shumailov's variance tracking formula across different architectures and test Guo's optimal mixture ratios in real-world scenarios.

evidence_inventory.title: Solutions and Future Work

core_papers.title: Is Model Collapse Inevitable? Breaking the Curse of Recursion by Accumulating Real and Synthetic Data
Self-Consuming Generative Models with Curated Data Provably Optimize Human Preferences
core_papers.key_finding: Proper curation turns bug into feature

core_papers.title: The Power of Noise: Redefining Retrieval for RAG Systems

evidence_inventory.narrative: Solutions emerge on three fronts: Gerstgrasser et al. show data accumulation strategies work, Padmanabhan et al. demonstrate curation can turn the problem into an optimization opportunity, and Cuconasu et al. provide architectural solutions through noise injection. Together these suggest a multi-pronged approach to preventing collapse.
